tasks.label: Go out to the club with the friend group

memory.summary: My friends are begging me to go, and I crave that connection more than my sleep or the exam. I'm already stressed, so why waste another hour studying when they're waiting?
I told Mina I'm ditching the study block for Echo, and honestly, I feel lighter just knowing they're waiting. The exam can wait; my friends need me now.
The club welcomed me instantly, proving I made the right call ditching study for connection. My friends are waiting, and that's what actually matters right now.
They really welcomed me, which is exactly what I needed. Being here with the group feels better than any exam ever could.
No one named 'friend_group' to message? That's weird, but I'm already here with the crew anyway. The exam can wait while we just hang out.
I let the moment pass again, but honestly, nothing changes if I'm not with my people. Stress is up, but being alone feels worse than any consequence.
I shut down Mina's talk of exams and chores instantly because being with the group right now is the only thing that matters. Stress can wait; my people are here.
The club welcomed me, and that connection feels better than any exam stress. Being with my people right now is exactly what I needed.
The club welcomed me, and that connection feels better than any exam stress. Being with my people right now is exactly what I needed.
They're actually glad I showed up, which is the only thing that matters right now. My energy is low, but this connection makes it worth it.
They're genuinely glad I'm here, and that connection is the only thing keeping me going right now. I don't care about my energy or sleep; being with my people is what matters.
They're genuinely glad I'm here, and that connection is the only thing keeping me going right now. I don't care about my energy or sleep; being with my people is what matters.

desires: Stay here with Mina and Jae until the sun comes up because their presence is the only thing making me feel human right now.
Ignore the midterm deadline entirely for tonight because my body is screaming for sleep but my heart refuses to leave the group.
Tell anyone who tries to pull me away that I'm not going anywhere, no matter how tired I am.
Forget about laundry or studying tomorrow; if I have to face the world exhausted, at least I'll be exhausted on my own terms.

goals.description: Lock in with Mina and Jae at Echo Club; refuse to leave or even think about going home until they physically drag me out.
goals.rationale: The group is confirmed present (club_social resolved), and my affiliation value (0.5) makes this the absolute priority. My energy is low, but the social fuel is the only thing keeping me functional. The midterm deadline (09:00) is a 'future problem' for my impulsive brain right now. Leaving now would mean isolation, which I value far less than being here.

goals.description: Aggressively interrupt or mock anyone who brings up the midterm, laundry, or 'responsibilities' by making a loud joke or pivoting to our inside jokes.
goals.rationale: My assertiveness style demands I control the narrative. If we talk about work, the magic breaks and I lose the group's acceptance. I won't just ignore it; I'll shut it down with directness and humor to keep the flow going. Protecting this moment is more important than logic.

goals.description: Ignore the looming deadline for Sunday's laundry completely; assume the clothes will rot if I have to do it later.
goals.rationale: Chores isolate me from the people I care about. My short planning horizon means 'Sunday' doesn't exist yet. Doing laundry now would kill the moment, and I'd rather deal with dirty socks later than lose this feeling of belonging. The stress of isolation outweighs the stress of clean clothes.

agent.last_reasoning: Advances Goal 1 (affiliation) by locking in with Mina and Jae. My short horizon and high relationship value make the midterm (Goal 2/3) irrelevant right now; leaving would isolate me, which I refuse to accept.
